body:
## Description

Seeing this warning a lot on some Holesky nodes with 200 peers:

```
Oct 02 02:05:43.029 WARN  Could not publish message                     error: NoPeersSubscribedToTopic, kind: beacon_attestation_5
```

## Version

v8.0.0-rc.0

## Present Behaviour

The beacon node doesn't have sufficient peers on the attestation subnets its publishing to. This is likely caused by a recent change (#7915), which prioritises uniform distribution across data column subnets instead of attestation subnets from the Fulu fork.

## Expected Behaviour

The beacon node should have sufficient peers on its required attestation subnets most of the time and the frequency of this warning should be low.

labels.description: Something isn't working
labels.name: bug

labels.description: Q4 2025 Fusaka Mainnet Release
labels.name: v8.0.0

title: Could not publish attestation message due to `NoPeersSubscribedToTopic`

body:
I think we should pause light client computations if the fork epoch is misaligned in a way that breaks our calculations.

This should be necessary only for ~1 sync committee period around the fork.

It would be nice to avoid error logs, even if they are harmless, like:

> Feb 13 20:25:36.228 ERRO error computing light_client updates LightClientUpdateError(BeaconStateError(IncorrectStateVariant)), service: lc_update

labels.name: bug

labels.name: v8.0.0

title: Resolve light client errors at fork boundaries

body:
## Issue Addressed

Follow-up to the bug fixed in:

- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/8121

This fixes the root cause of that bug, which was introduced by me in:

- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/8101

Jimmy and Lion both identified the issue here:

- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/8101#discussion_r2382258842
- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/8101#discussion_r2382710356

## Proposed Changes

In the methods that compute the proposer shuffling decision root, ensure we don't use lookahead for the Fulu fork epoch itself. This is accomplished by checking if Fulu is enabled at `epoch - 1`, i.e. if `epoch > fulu_fork_epoch`.

I haven't updated the methods that _compute_ shufflings to use these new corrected bounds (e.g. `BeaconState::compute_proposer_indices`), although we could make this change in future. The `get_beacon_proposer_indices` method already gracefully handles the Fulu boundary case by using the `proposer_lookahead` field (if initialised).

## Additional Info

- [x] Unit test added for `proposer_shuffling_decision_slot` at the boundary
- [x] Updated the consistency tests to check cases around the fork boundary

title: Fix proposer shuffling decision slot at boundary

body:
## Issue Addressed

N/A

## Proposed Changes

In #8101 , when we modified the logic to get the proposer index post fulu, we seem to have missed advancing the state at the fork boundaries to get the right `Fork` for signature verification. 
This led to lighthouse failing all gossip verification right after transitioning to fulu that was observed on the holesky shadow fork
```
Sep 26 14:24:00.088 DEBUG Rejected gossip block                         error: "InvalidSignature(ProposerSignature)", graffiti: "grandine-geth-super-1", slot: 640 
Sep 26 14:24:00.099 WARN  Could not verify block for gossip. Rejecting the block  error: InvalidSignature(ProposerSignature)
```

I'm not completely sure this is the correct fix, but this fixes the issue with `InvalidProposerSignature` on the holesky shadow fork.

Thanks to @eserilev for helping debug this

body:
## Issue Addressed

As identified by a researcher during the Fusaka security competition, we were computing the proposer index incorrectly in some places by computing without lookahead.

## Proposed Changes

- [x] Add "low level" checks to computation functions in `consensus/types` to ensure they error cleanly
- [x] Re-work the determination of proposer shuffling decision roots, which are now fork aware.
- [x] Re-work and simplify the beacon proposer cache to be fork-aware.
- [x] Optimise `with_proposer_cache` to use `OnceCell`.
- [x] All tests passing.
- [x] Resolve all remaining `FIXME(sproul)`s.
- [x] Unit tests for `ProtoBlock::proposer_shuffling_root_for_child_block`.
- [x] End-to-end regression test.
- [x] Test on pre-Fulu network.
- [x] Test on post-Fulu network.

## Testing

- The correctness of `ProtoBlock::proposer_shuffling_root_for_child_block` is tested by new tests added in e4713156e1c1013cf720540e8125794362588d99. They catch the bug that was fixed by 83128c40ef5d10b04906c0ad20fad23e69e57117.

body:
## Description

On Holesky, starting the beacon node with `--checkpoint-sync-url` on **Lighthouse v7.1.0** consistently fails to start. Representative error:

Failed to start beacon node reason: "Error loading checkpoint state from remote: InvalidSsz(OffsetSkipsVariableBytes(2737225))"

Tried multiple public checkpoint providers (EthPandaOps, ChainSafe, BeaconState, SIGP). Some returned different errors (500/502/DNS). After upgrading Lighthouse to **v8.0.0-rc.0**, checkpoint sync **succeeds** immediately with the same setup.

## Version

- Lighthouse (failing): `v7.1.0-cfb1f73` (built from source)
- Lighthouse (working): `v8.0.0-rc.0-e5b4983`
- Rust/Cargo: `rustc 1.90.0`, `cargo 1.90.0` (via rustup)
- OS: Ubuntu 22.04 (Jammy), amd64
- Execution client: Geth `1.16.3-stable (d818a9af)`; Engine API on `127.0.0.1:8551`

## Present Behaviour

Launching `lighthouse bn` with a checkpoint URL on **v7.1.0** exits with one of the following (depending on provider/time):

CRIT Failed to start beacon node reason: "Error loading checkpoint state from remote: InvalidSsz(OffsetSkipsVariableBytes(2737225))"
CRIT Failed to start beacon node reason: "ServerMessage(ErrorMessage { code: 500, message: "no finality known" ... })"
CRIT Failed to start beacon node reason: "StatusCode(502)"
CRIT Failed to start beacon node reason: "HttpClient(... dns error: failed to lookup address information ...)"



Meanwhile, Geth logs show:

WARN Beacon client online, but no consensus updates received ...

After upgrading to **v8.0.0-rc.0**, Lighthouse logs:

INFO Starting checkpoint sync remote_url: [https://checkpoint-sync.holesky.ethpandaops.io/](https://checkpoint-sync.holesky.ethpandaops.io/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

Geth then begins syncing (forkchoice updates received).

## Expected Behaviour

With a valid `--checkpoint-sync-url`, Lighthouse should load a finalized Holesky checkpoint and begin following the chain, driving forkchoice on the execution client.

## Steps to resolve

- **Workaround that fixed it for me:** upgrade Lighthouse to **v8.0.0-rc.0** (built from git tag), then run checkpoint sync again.
- Happy to:
  - Re-run **v7.1.0** with `RUST_LOG=debug` and share full logs.
  - Test against a known-good pinned checkpoint (state/block SSZ) if provided.
  - Try any specific flags you recommend.

---

### Minimal Repro

```bash
# Execution Layer (Geth)
geth --holesky --datadir ~/ethereum/execution/holesky --syncmode snap \
  --http --http.addr 127.0.0.1 --http.port 8545 --http.api eth,net,web3,txpool

# Consensus Layer (v7.1.0 fails, v8.0.0-rc.0 works)
lighthouse bn --network holesky \
  --datadir ~/ethereum/consensus/holesky \
  --execution-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:8551 \
  --execution-jwt ~/ethereum/execution/holesky/geth/jwtsecret \
  --checkpoint-sync-url https://checkpoint-sync.holesky.ethpandaops.io/
title: Holesky: Lighthouse v7.1.0 fails to start with checkpoint sync (InvalidSsz…); upgrading to v8.0.0-rc.0 fixes

body:
During backfill the only verification we do is of the block's signatures:

https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/blob/d96b73152e0e4bcedb8e747016ba8070029db50d/beacon_node/beacon_chain/src/historical_blocks.rs#L205-L213

This does not cover the blobs or data columns, which could be mismatched/invalid.

For blobs we should check:

- [x] KZG inclusion proof (already checked by the network crate: https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/blob/d96b73152e0e4bcedb8e747016ba8070029db50d/beacon_node/network/src/sync/network_context/requests/blobs_by_range.rs#L36).
- [x] Blob data matches the KZG commitment: TODO (verify KZG proof).
- [ ] Signature in the blob's block header matches the block's signature (equality check is fine).

Similarly for data columns.

body:
## Description

This is a tracking issue for the work that is being undertaken on building out Data Availability Sampling.

This will be updated and edited as we go.

Instructions for running a DAS devnet can be found in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/jimmygchen/54574443ea8a94118f0f748b69a5e610).

Main PR to `unstable`: 
- #5683 

## Devnet 5

- [x] #6251
- [ ] #6258
- [x] #5990 
- [x] #6268 
- [x] #5894
- [x] #6111
- [x] #6018 
- [ ] #6107 
- [ ] Implement validator custody ([spec](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/3779)) https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/6767
- [x] https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/6854
- [ ] Address all `TODO(das)` comments
- [ ] Batch verify KZG proofs where possible https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/6851
- [x] Don't lookup sync individual columns received from gossip. WIP  [`dapplion:dedup-columns-by-root-requests`](https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/compare/unstable...dapplion:lighthouse:dedup-columns-by-root-requests?expand=1)

## `TODO(das)`

- Collection of small / easy TODOs https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/6862
- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/6850

## Initial implementation

### Data format and distribution (#5050)

- [x] Add `ColumnSidecar` type 
- [x] Extend blobs horizontally with dummy data (`0x{random_uniq_bits}...{col_id}`) and distribute to column subnets
- [x] Create 32 column subnets `NUM_COLUMN_SUBNETS`
- [x] Compute subnets to subscribe to based on `node_id` (see att example [here](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/dev/specs/phase0/p2p-interface.md#attestation-subnet-subscription))
- [x] Node to subscribe to only `CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT` column subnets

### Data Column Custody

- [x] #5196
- [x] Advertise extra custodied columns through ENR (@jacobkaufmann)
- [x] Implement `PEERDAS_ACTIVATION_EPOCH` to transition from deneb blobs to data columns (@jimmygchen)
- [x] #5570
- [x] #5684
- [x] ❌ Proposer to store all data columns to prevent data loss (See [this comment](https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/5570#discussion_r1575807617)) - increase peer count and custody requirement instead

### Sampling

- [x] #5196
- [x] #5616
- [x] #5569
- [ ] Don't attempt downloading sample for custody columns already in DB
- [x] #6929
- [x] #6930

### Syncing

- [x] #5353
- [x] #5747
- [x] #5776

### Misc 

- [x] #5210
- [x] #5701
- [x] ❌ #5734
- [x]  #5772 
- [x]  #5750
- [x] #5781 
- [x] #5783
- [x] #5779
- [x] #5805 
- [x] #5828
- [x] #5890

### `peerdas-devnet-1`

- [x] #5950 

### `peerdas-devnet-2`

- [x] #6233
- [x] #6289 

### `peerdas-devnet-3`

- [x] #6072 
- [x] #6026
- [x] #6109

### Post-Pectra (Sampling, 2D PeerDAS etc)

- [ ] Update fork choice with sampling results ([spec](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/3779))
- [x] #6110 

## Current behaviour not specified by consensus-specs

- _Only supernode_ performs reconsturctions once >50% of columns received
- LossyDAS implementation
- Re-process `DataColumnsByRoot` requests and respond when columns are ready

body:
## Issue Addressed

- [ ] Remove penalties on failed block sidecar coupling as this is problematic in forky situations. Add them to fail peer only and deprioritise them instead.
  - May no longer be required with the new range sync approach, as we now query columns by root.
- [x] Add logic to maintain custody column peers and prevent pruning
  - #7915
- [x] Prioritise status v2
  - #7912
- [ ] Penalise peers for sending blocks containing invalid execution payload.
- [x] Only send lookup request to synced / advanced peers
  - #7913

title: Custody persist fix

body:
## Issue Addressed

Closes:

- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/7489

## Proposed Changes

Use `ForkName::latest_stable`, i.e. Electra when decoding blobs from a server that does not provide `version`. This is only a temporary workaround that should be reverted once `checkpointz` is fixed. Having a default fork is potentially incorrect, and glossing over bugs in servers in general is not ideal.

However, even in the case where we update `ForkName::latest_stable` to `Fulu`, this should continue to work, as the blob limit is likely to increase and the `RuntimeVariableList` will just have a slightly higher limit than necessary (which is OK so long as the server isn't buggy enough to violate the correct lower bound: e.g. if the block is an Electra one and the server sends 10 blobs, which exceeds the Electra max (9) but not the Fulu max).

## Additional Info

I've opened an issue to revert this PR before we proceed with Fulu on mainnet, but _after_ v7.1.0:

- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/7496

body:
## Issue Addressed

Partially addresses 
- https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/6258

## Proposed Changes

TBD

1000 lines are for new unit tests :)

## Questions / TODO

- Should custody_by_range requests try all possible peers before giving up? i.e. should they ignore the failures counter for custody failures? Add a test for this behaviour.

**Tests to add**

- Peer does not send columns at all on specific index
- We find no-one serving columns on a specific index, how to recover
- Random tests where most peer are faulty and we have to keep cycling them. Use randomness and run the tests with different levels of fault % :=> build a simulator
- Permanently fail a single column many times, but then resolve the rest such that we can reconstruct.
- Send non-matching data columns
- Test downscoring of custody failures
- Test the fallback mechanism of peer selection
- Test a syncing chain reaching 0 peers, both for forward sync and backwards sync
- Test forwards sync on Fulu with blocks without data

### Future TODOs

- Ensure uniform use of `subscribe_all_data_column_subnets` to determine `is_supernode`
- Ensure consistent naming in sync network context handlers `on_xxx_response`, suggestion -> https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/pull/7510#discussion_r2107334518
- Cleanup `add_peer_*` functions in sync tests. Best to do in another PR as it touches a lot of lookup sync tests (unrelated) 
- Track `requests_per_peer` to ensure that eventually we fetch data from all peers in the chain

title: Fix test OOM issues on tree-states-hot

body:
## Description

The initial Fulu spec was merged in https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/3994, and `EIP7594_FORK_EPOCH` and `EIP7594_FORK_VERSION` no longer exists in the configuration; The spec now specifies PeerDAS to be activated at `FULU_FORK_EPOCH`.

We'll need to change PeerDAS activation to happen at the Fulu fork in Lighthouse, and remove the `eip7594` related configurations. There is a WIP PR for the Fulu fork boiler plate, and is required for this task.

labels.description: Data Availability Sampling
labels.name: das

title: Activate PeerDAS at Fulu Fork and remove `EIP7594_FORK_EPOCH`

body:
## Issue Addressed

From PeerDAS local testing I noticed some errors with failing to send computed columns to receiver:

https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/blob/dd7591f7123dfe072631c0deb0abc1b78cc82733/beacon_node/beacon_chain/src/fetch_blobs.rs#L252

I believe there is a race condition, the sequence:
1. Fetch EL blobs triggered, received 4 blobs from the EL, triggered column computation
1. All 8 custody columns received via gossip
1. Columns written to db, block imported. (Column receiver dropped)
1. Computed columns but fail to send as receiver was dropped.

We should not even try to compute the columns if we've already imported the block from gossip. This consumes extra resources and bandwidth and is unnecessary.
